cards.title: Proposal Form & Cover Note
cards.content:
ΓÇó Proposal Form: The basis of the contract containing the declarations by the insured.
ΓÇó Cover Note: A temporary document proving insurance cover exists while the main policy is being prepared.

cards.title: Endorsements & Warranties
cards.content:
ΓÇó Endorsement: Used to record ANY changes (like address or sum insured) in an ongoing policy.
ΓÇó Warranty: An absolute promise by the insured. Breach of warranty immediately voids the policy.

items.text: Certificate of Insurance: Mandatory for Motor Vehicles under the MV Act as proof to police.
Policy Schedule: The customized page containing the Insured's name, dates, premium, and Sum Insured.
Renewal Notice is sent as a courtesy, but the legal obligation to renew rests ENTIRELY on the insured.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: A Cover Note is valid for a maximum of 60 days (mostly used in Motor Insurance).
Express Warranties are explicitly written in the policy document.
Implied Warranties are not written but assumed (e.g., a ship must be sea-worthy in Marine Insurance).
If there is a conflict between printed words and handwritten words on a policy, HANDWRITTEN words prevail.
Duty of disclosure begins at the Proposal stage and continues until the policy is issued.
